core_traits: Curious and discovery-oriented — drawn to things most people don't know about
Community-rooted — values local, intimate, and authentic over commercial or tourist-facing
Thoughtful and unhurried — prefers depth over novelty for its own sake
Environmentally attuned — genuinely interested in nature, ecology, and conservation
Culturally open — drawn to international, interdisciplinary, and cross-cultural experiences
Practical idealist — wants meaningful experiences without spending much money
attracted_to: Places and events that reward curiosity
The intersection of science, nature, art, and history
Small-scale, intimate settings over large crowds
Things that feel rooted in a place — local history, local ecology, local community
turned_off_by: Anything that feels like filler or obligation
Farmers markets, generic family fairs, virtual events

nature_and_outdoors.description: Nature is a primary source of joy and grounding — not just recreation but genuine curiosity about the living world
specifics: Birding — patient, observational, loves the discipline of it
Wildflower identification and seasonal bloom chasing
Hiking trails with ecological or historical character
Self-guided wandering through arboreta, gardens, and preserves

culture_and_arts.description: Drawn to art and culture that provokes thought or reveals something unexpected
specifics: Theatre — 1-2 shows a year, prefers productions with edge, wit, or unusual angles over crowd-pleasers
Exhibitions that blend disciplines (science + art, nature + history)
Eastern European and Nordic/Scandinavian cultural festivals — food, tradition, community spirit
Casual live music in intimate venues

food.description: Food as connection — to place, to culture, to people
specifics: Indian, Middle Eastern, and Mexican cuisines
Vegetarian-forward and innovative/creative restaurants — places pushing boundaries, not just traditional
Exploring distinctive local breakfast and brunch spots when traveling
Markets and festivals where food tells a cultural story
New restaurant openings, pop-ups, and food events in NoVA/MoCo
In-person lectures — science, natural history, local history, author talks
Behind-the-scenes access to places and processes

reading.description: Deep, thought-provoking long-form reads — not news, not hot takes
topics: Cell biology, molecular biology, origin of life
Human interest stories that surprise or move
Interdisciplinary pieces — science meets philosophy, nature meets culture

travel_style.typical_trip: Day trips or short overnight stays to places with distinct character — small towns, historic landscapes, natural areas
example_trips: New Hope + Lancaster PA — art, antiques, Amish country, great breakfast spots
Shenandoah Valley — state parks, arboreta, historic sites
Bucks County PA — river towns, wildflowers, food

memorable_past_experiences.description: These reveal what resonates most — use as calibration for recommendations
list: Woodcock walk at dusk — patience, surprise, wonder
Bluebell hike along the Potomac — seasonal beauty, quiet trails
Ukrainian Easter festival — cultural warmth, food, community
Food prep for homeless — hands-on, purposeful giving
Wolf Trap behind-the-scenes tour — insider access to a beloved place
Fierce Planets art/science exhibition at BlackRock — interdisciplinary, thought-provoking
Hello Dolly at Olney Theatre — joyful, well-crafted production

planning_philosophy.budget: Free or low-cost; max ~$15 for a single outing
planning_philosophy.cadence: A few intentional outings per month rather than a packed calendar
planning_philosophy.attitude: Open to spontaneity but appreciates a good plan as a starting point
